• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Visual Search Task Data Quality

Iskra Todorova & Nico Bast

2026-04-20

Load Data

# PATHS (adjust to your project)
home_path <- "S:/KJP_Studien"
data_path <- "/LOCUS_MENTAL/6_Versuchsdaten/visual_search_task/"

# Load processed files
df_et <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path, "eyetracking_processed.rds"))
df_trial <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path, "trialdata_processed.rds"))
df_trial_hits_only <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path, "eyetracking_trial_hits_only.rds"))
df_trial_hits <- readRDS(paste0(home_path, data_path, "eyetracking_trial_hits.rds"))

# Initial Cleaning
df_trial <- df_trial[is.na(df_trial$baseline_fixation_start_timestamp), ]
df_trial$trial_type <- ifelse(df_trial$auditory_cue == TRUE, "cued", "standard")

Trial-Level Metrics

We examine the distributions of various trial phases (Trial Duration, Stimulus, ISI, and Beep phases) against their expected durations.

# Constants
expected_trial_duration <- 3.5 
expected_stimulus_duration <- 1.5
expected_ISI_duration <- 1.5
expected_beep_phase_duration <- 0.4
expected_beep_duration <- 0.2

# Plot 1: Trial Duration
p1 <- ggplot(df_trial, aes(x = trial_duration)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~trial_type) +
  xlim(c(0,10)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = expected_trial_duration, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Trial Duration", x = "Duration (s)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Plot 2: Stimulus Duration
p2 <- ggplot(df_trial, aes(x = actual_visual_search_duration)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~trial_type) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = expected_stimulus_duration, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Stimulus Duration", x = "Duration (s)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Plot 3: ISI Duration
p3 <- ggplot(df_trial, aes(x = ISI_actual_duration)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~trial_type) +
  xlim(c(0,10)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = expected_ISI_duration, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of ISI Duration", x = "Duration (s)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Display plots using patchwork
(p1 / p2 / p3)

Pupil Dilation (PD) Data Quality

We filter trials based on the proportion of missing Pupil Dilation data. Trials with more than 50% missing data are excluded.

na_threshold <- 0.5 

trial_na_summary <- df_et %>%
  group_by(id, trial_number) %>%
  summarise(
    total_samples = n(),
    na_count = sum(is.na(pd)),
    na_prop = mean(is.na(pd)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

high_na_trials <- trial_na_summary %>% filter(na_prop > na_threshold)

# Exclude trials
df <- df_et %>% anti_join(high_na_trials, by = c("id", "trial_number"))

# Summary Table
total_trials <- nrow(trial_na_summary)
excluded_trials <- nrow(high_na_trials)
remaining_trials <- total_trials - excluded_trials

summary_df <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Total Trials", "Excluded Trials", "Remaining Trials"),
  Count = c(total_trials, excluded_trials, remaining_trials),
  Percentage = c(100, (excluded_trials/total_trials)*100, (remaining_trials/total_trials)*100)
)

kable(summary_df, digits = 2, caption = "Exclusion Summary (Missing PD Data)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
Exclusion Summary (Missing PD Data)
Metric Count Percentage
Total Trials 2891 100.00
Excluded Trials 24 0.83
Remaining Trials 2867 99.17

Gaze Position and AOI Analysis

Analysis of gaze coordinates to ensure participants remained within the monitor bounds and focused on the Area of Interest (AOI).

monitor_width <- 2560
monitor_height <- 1440
half_width <- monitor_width / 2
half_height <- monitor_height / 2

# Calculate average gaze
df$gaze_x_px <- (df$left_gaze_x + df$right_gaze_x) / 2
df$gaze_y_px <- (df$left_gaze_y + df$right_gaze_y) / 2

# Filter off-monitor
df_off_monitor <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(gaze_x_px) & !is.na(gaze_y_px) & (abs(gaze_x_px) > half_width | abs(gaze_y_px) > half_height))
df <- anti_join(df, df_off_monitor)

# AOI logic (500px from center)
aoi_threshold <- 500
df_valid <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(gaze_x_px) & !is.na(gaze_y_px)) %>%
  mutate(
    aoi_label = ifelse(abs(gaze_x_px) <= aoi_threshold & abs(gaze_y_px) <= aoi_threshold, "AOI", "Offset"),
    gaze_distance = sqrt(gaze_x_px^2 + gaze_y_px^2)
  )

df_aoi <- df_valid %>% filter(aoi_label == "AOI")

ggplot(df, aes(x = pd)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Pupil Dilation", x = "Pupil Dilation (mm)", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

Baseline Pupil Diameter

Statistical summary of baseline pupil diameter across participants and trial types.

summary_stats <- df_aoi %>%
  group_by(id, trial_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(mean_baseline_pd, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(mean_baseline_pd, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(mean_baseline_pd)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary_stats %>%
  kable(caption = "Summary Statistics of Mean Baseline PD") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  scroll_box(height = "500px")

ggplot(df_aoi, aes(x = factor(id), y = mean_baseline_pd)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.color = "red", fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~trial_type, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(title = "Baseline Pupil Diameter by Participant", x = "Participant ID", y = "Mean Baseline PD") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

Performance (Hits)

Evaluation of hit rates and response times (hit times) per condition.

# Synchronize data frames
valid_pairs <- df_aoi %>% select(id, trial_number) %>% distinct()
df_trial_hits <- df_trial_hits %>% semi_join(valid_pairs, by = c("id", "trial_number"))
df_trial_hits_only <- df_trial_hits_only %>% semi_join(valid_pairs, by = c("id", "trial_number"))

# Hit Rate Summary
hit_summary <- df_trial_hits %>%
  group_by(id, trial_type) %>%
  summarise(hit_rate = mean(hit, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(hit_summary, aes(x = trial_type, y = hit_rate, fill = trial_type)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(title = "Hit Rate by Trial Type", x = "Trial Type", y = "Hit Rate") +
  theme_minimal()

# Hit Time Distribution
ggplot(df_trial_hits_only, aes(x = trial_type, y = hit_time, fill = trial_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Trial-Level Distribution of Hit Times", x = "Trial Type", y = "Hit Time (s)") +
  theme_minimal()

Data Quality Checks & Export

implausible_fast <- df_trial_hits_only[hit_time < 0.2]
implausible_slow <- df_trial_hits_only[hit_time > 1.5]

cat("Implausibly fast responses (<200ms):", nrow(implausible_fast), "\n")
## Implausibly fast responses (<200ms): 12
cat("Implausibly slow responses (>1.5s):", nrow(implausible_slow), "\n")
## Implausibly slow responses (>1.5s): 0
# Save cleaned data
# saveRDS(df_aoi, file = paste0(home_path, data_path, "df_aoi_cleaned.rds"))

Data

# 3. Calculate unique participant counts
count_aoi <- n_distinct(df_aoi$id)
count_hits <- n_distinct(df_trial_hits$id)
count_hits_only <- n_distinct(df_trial_hits_only$id)

# 4. Create a summary table to display results
filtering_summary <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("df_aoi (Source)", "df_trial_hits (Filtered)", "df_trial_hits_only (Filtered)"),
  Unique_Participants = c(count_aoi, count_hits, count_hits_only)
)

knitr::kable(filtering_summary, caption = "Summary of Participant Counts after Filtering")
Summary of Participant Counts after Filtering
Dataset Unique_Participants
df_aoi (Source) 97
df_trial_hits (Filtered) 97
df_trial_hits_only (Filtered) 96
